task_completion.description: Visit check.torproject.org to verify Tor connection and Exit IP
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tor Browser is open and navigated to check.torproject.org (or a similar IP checking site), displaying the Tor connection status and an IP address.

task_completion.description: Check the Tor Browser application version and User Agent
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tor Browser is open to 'about:support', 'about:preferences', or the 'About Tor Browser' dialog, showing the browser version and User Agent string.

task_completion.description: Check installed extensions and NoScript status
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tor Browser is open to 'about:addons' or the extensions menu is expanded, showing NoScript and its enabled/disabled status.

task_completion.description: Check the Security Level and HTTPS-Only Mode status
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tor Browser is open to the Privacy & Security settings page or the Security Shield menu is expanded, showing the current Security Level (Standard/Safer/Safest) and HTTPS-Only Mode settings.

task_completion.description: Open a text editor to create the audit report at the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, vim, or mousepad) is open, and the window title or file path indicates it is saving to '/home/ga/Documents/tor-audit-report.txt'.

task_completion.description: Compile all findings into the report with the required structure and headers
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the required headers (e.g., '--- Application Info ---', '--- Security Settings ---') and the gathered data (version, IP, security level, etc.) filled in correctly.

integrity.description: The agent used the Tor Browser GUI to gather information rather than guessing or using terminal scraping scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active navigation through Tor Browser menus, settings, and web pages.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely checked the Exit IP address via the browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is at least one screenshot showing a webpage (like check.torproject.org) that displays the current IP address, rather than the agent hallucinating an IP.

integrity.description: The data entered into the report matches the data seen in the browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The version number, Security Level, and Exit IP written in the text editor match the values visible in the browser screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The final report must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/tor-audit-report.txt'. It must contain exactly these 5 section headers: '--- Application Info ---', '--- Installed Extensions ---', '--- Security Settings ---', '--- Tor Network Verification ---', and '--- Audit Metadata ---'. The agent is expected to visit 'check.torproject.org' in the Tor Browser to verify the Tor connection and Exit IP.
